sizing_rule.note: Dynamic fractional f recalculates position sizing as account equity grows, achieving faster growth than static fractional f. Example: dynamic f achieves 11x TWR in 125.2458 days vs static f requiring 269 days at 0.1 fractional f. Adjust position size periodically based on equity changes while maintaining initial risk level.

source_rule_text: Use dynamic fractional f position sizing adjusted periodically based on account equity changes, rather than static fractional f, to achieve faster account growth with the same initial risk level.
